mnemonics: Repeat and Reflect: RNNs Repeat previous inputs and Reflect on past outputs.
Lock-Step Update: LSNNs use Lock-Step updates to update the hidden state.
keyPoints: Definition of RNNs: RNNs are a type of neural network designed for sequential data.
Types of RNNs: There are three types of RNNs: Simple RNN, LSTM, and GRU.
Advantages of RNNs: RNNs are useful for modeling sequential data, such as natural language processing and speech recognition.
